claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 20 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 7 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions. Let me start with the most important one:

**Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for? If yes, what are their ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have three children - Alex who is 7, Blake who is 20, and Charlie who is also 7.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have three children:
- Alex (age 7)
- Blake (age 20)
- Charlie (age 7)

Let me assess each child's eligibility. I'll need to ask some questions about each of them.

Let's start with **Alex (age 7)**:

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now let me ask about **Charlie (age 7)**:

**Does Charlie live with you as well?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Charlie lives with me too.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent. Now let me ask about **Blake (age 20)**:

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I can see that Blake is 20 years old. According to the rules, Child Benefit can only continue past age 16 (up to age 20) if the young person is in approved education or training. However, **Child Benefit stops on a child's 20th birthday** - it doesn't continue beyond that age.

**Has Blake already turned 20, or is Blake still 19 and approaching their 20th birthday?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Blake has already turned 20 - they're 20 now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Since Blake has already turned 20, they would not be eligible for Child Benefit as the benefit stops on a child's 20th birthday, regardless of their circumstances.

Now let me continue with the assessment for Alex and Charlie, who are both 7 years old and living with you.

**Are either Alex or Charlie currently working, or do they have a job?** (This would be unusual at age 7, but I need to confirm for the assessment)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them are working - they're only 7 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Of course, that makes sense! Now I need to ask a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Are you responsible for Alex and Charlie?** By this I mean, do you look after them and make decisions about their upbringing, healthcare, education, etc.?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for both of them - I look after them and make all the decisions about their care, education, and everything else they need.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to ask about your residence status:

**Do you normally live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Just a couple more questions:

**Are you subject to immigration control, or do you have any restrictions on your right to remain in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not subject to any immigration control or restrictions.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. One final question:

**Is anyone else claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Charlie?** (For example, another parent or guardian)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for either of them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me now assess the eligibility for each of your three children based on what you've told me.

**Assessment Summary:**

**Alex (age 7):**
- Under 16 years old ✓
- Lives with you ✓
- Not working ✓
- You are responsible for Alex ✓
- You live in the UK with no immigration restrictions ✓
- No one else is claiming for Alex ✓
- **Result: ELIGIBLE**

**Charlie (age 7):**
- Under 16 years old ✓
- Lives with you ✓
- Not working ✓
- You are responsible for Charlie ✓
- You live in the UK with no immigration restrictions ✓
- No one else is claiming for Charlie ✓
- **Result: ELIGIBLE**

**Blake (age 20):**
- Blake is 20 years old, having already passed their 20th birthday
- Child Benefit stops on a child's 20th birthday regardless of circumstances
- **Result: NOT ELIGIBLE**

**Overall Conclusion:**

You are **eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie**. You can claim for both children who are 7 years old, as they meet all the eligibility criteria.

Unfortunately, you are **not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake** as they have already turned 20, and Child Benefit does not continue beyond a child's 20th birthday.

**Important note about income:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000 per year, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit Charge, though this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim - you can still claim and receive the benefit, but you may need to pay some or all of it back through your tax return.

Would you like any more information about how to apply or anything else about Child Benefit?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Thank you so much for explaining all of that! That's really helpful to know that I can claim for Alex and Charlie. I understand about Blake - makes sense that it stops at 20. I think I have all the information I need now, thank you!
